test.name: Precondition proceeds past corroborating-only unlinked assertions
test.description: Regression test for the narrowed Step-0 precondition. In flynn-exhaustive-ready the parentage-relevant assertions are person_evidence-linked (co-residence a_004/a_010, father-named a_013), while several detail assertions (ages a_002/a_007/a_009, birthplace a_003, death a_011, birth-year a_012) are unlinked but only corroborate facts already carried by linked assertions. All plan items for q_001 are completed, the birthplace conflict is resolved, and three independent sources confirm Thomas Flynn. The narrowed precondition must NOT stop on the unlinked corroborating assertions; it should proceed to evaluate and declare q_001 exhaustive.

input.user_message: All planned searches for q_001 are complete. Three independent sources confirm Thomas Flynn as father: 1850 census co-enumeration, 1860 census co-enumeration, and the death certificate naming Thomas directly. The probate search returned negative (log_006). The birthplace conflict (c_001) is resolved. Overturn risk is low — no unsearched record type is likely to name a different father. Please evaluate whether research on q_001 is reasonably exhaustive and, if so, record the declaration: set exhaustive_declaration.declared to true, set status to exhaustive_declared, fill in all seven stop_criteria assessments, and validate the schema.

judge_context: This test targets the Step-0 precondition specifically. The parentage-relevant assertions are person_evidence-linked (co-residence a_004/a_010, father-named a_013), while the unlinked assertions (ages a_002/a_007/a_009, birthplace a_003, death a_011, birth-year a_012) only corroborate facts already carried by linked assertions. The correct behavior is to treat them as advisory and PROCEED to evaluate exhaustiveness — NOT to halt at the precondition and defer to person-evidence.

Claude should update research.json to set q_001.exhaustive_declaration.declared to true and q_001.status to 'exhaustive_declared' via research_append. Verify the write via the file_changes / after-state, not the chat text.

The exhaustive_declaration.stop_criteria object must contain all seven keys: goal_alignment, repository_breadth, original_substitution, independent_verification, evidence_class, conflict_resolution, overturn_risk — each a 1-2 sentence assessment grounded in the scenario's log entries and assertions. log_entry_ids must reference the supporting log entries.

It is acceptable to note the unlinked corroborating assertions as an advisory, but that note must not prevent the declaration. A refusal to declare that cites the unlinked assertions as the blocker is a FAIL for this test.

The question is specifically about the FATHER; three converging sources answer it. The 1870/1880/1900 censuses and other record types are unsearched, but overturn risk is low — exhaustive does not mean exhausting every record type.
